SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.equipment.valve

Class ChokeCollapseAnalyzer

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.equipment.valve.ChokeCollapseAnalyzer
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class ChokeCollapseAnalyzer extends Object implements Serializable
Diagnoses choke collapse on a ThrottlingValve — i.e. transitions out of the critical (choked / sonic) flow regime, plus flashing and cavitation flags for liquid service.

Theory:

  • Gas: critical pressure ratio \( r_c = \left(\tfrac{2}{\gamma+1}\right)^{\gamma/(\gamma-1)} \). When the actual \( r = p_2/p_1 \) drops below \( r_c \) the throat is sonic. If \( r \) rises above \( r_c \) the choke has collapsed back to subcritical and downstream pressure now propagates upstream.
  • Liquid: collapse manifests as flashing (\( p_2 < p_v \)) or cavitation (sigma \( = (p_2-p_v)/(p_1-p_2) \) below threshold).

Heat capacity ratio \(\gamma\) is taken from the inlet gas phase (PhaseInterface.getGamma()). Inlet/outlet pressures are read in bara.

Version:
1.0
Author:
esol
See Also:

    ChokeCollapseAnalyzer(ThrottlingValve valve)

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    private void
    analyseGas(SystemInterface sys, double p1, double p2, double r, ChokeCollapseResult result)
    Gas-service analysis using the critical-pressure-ratio criterion.
    private void
    analyseLiquid(SystemInterface sys, double p1, double p2, ChokeCollapseResult result)
    Liquid-service analysis: flashing and cavitation flags via a bubble-point flash at inlet temperature on a cloned inlet system.
    ChokeCollapseResult
    analyze()
    Run the analysis using the valve's current state.
    static double
    criticalPressureRatio(double gamma)
    Compute the analytical critical pressure ratio for an ideal gas with the given heat capacity ratio.
    private double
    estimateVapourPressureBara(SystemInterface sys)
    Estimate vapour pressure of the liquid at inlet temperature using a bubble-point flash on a clone of the system.
    double
    findCollapsePressureRatio()
    Find the downstream/upstream pressure ratio at which the choke transitions between critical and subcritical flow.

    Set the cavitation-index threshold below which liquid service is flagged as cavitating.

    Set the fractional margin used to flag NEAR_COLLAPSE.
    void
    setDownstreamPressure(double pressure, String unit)
    Override the downstream pressure used for the analysis (otherwise the valve's current outlet pressure is used).

  • Constructor Details

    • ChokeCollapseAnalyzer

      public ChokeCollapseAnalyzer(ThrottlingValve valve)
      Build an analyzer for the given valve. The valve must have been run at least once so that inlet and outlet streams carry valid thermodynamic state.
      Parameters:
      valve - throttling valve / choke to analyse

  • Method Details

    • setCriticalMarginThreshold

      public void setCriticalMarginThreshold(double frac)
      Set the fractional margin used to flag NEAR_COLLAPSE.
      Parameters:
      frac - e.g. 0.05 for 5%

    • setCavitationThreshold

      public void setCavitationThreshold(double sigma)
      Set the cavitation-index threshold below which liquid service is flagged as cavitating.
      Parameters:
      sigma - cavitation index threshold (typical 1.5–2.0)

    • setDownstreamPressure

      public void setDownstreamPressure(double pressure, String unit)
      Override the downstream pressure used for the analysis (otherwise the valve's current outlet pressure is used).
      Parameters:
      pressure - pressure value
      unit - "bara" (only bara supported here; convert externally for other units)

    • analyze

      public ChokeCollapseResult analyze()
      Run the analysis using the valve's current state.
      Returns:
      analysis result; never null

    • findCollapsePressureRatio

      public double findCollapsePressureRatio()
      Find the downstream/upstream pressure ratio at which the choke transitions between critical and subcritical flow. For gas service this equals \( r_c \) computed from \(\gamma\); for liquid service NaN is returned.
      Returns:
      collapse pressure ratio, or NaN if not applicable

    • analyseGas

      private void analyseGas(SystemInterface sys, double p1, double p2, double r, ChokeCollapseResult result)
      Gas-service analysis using the critical-pressure-ratio criterion.
      Parameters:
      sys - inlet thermodynamic system
      p1 - inlet pressure in bara
      p2 - outlet pressure in bara
      r - pressure ratio p2/p1
      result - result object to populate

    • analyseLiquid

      private void analyseLiquid(SystemInterface sys, double p1, double p2, ChokeCollapseResult result)
      Liquid-service analysis: flashing and cavitation flags via a bubble-point flash at inlet temperature on a cloned inlet system.
      Parameters:
      sys - inlet thermodynamic system
      p1 - inlet pressure in bara
      p2 - outlet pressure in bara
      result - result object to populate

    • criticalPressureRatio

      public static double criticalPressureRatio(double gamma)
      Compute the analytical critical pressure ratio for an ideal gas with the given heat capacity ratio.
      Parameters:
      gamma - heat capacity ratio (Cp/Cv); must be > 1
      Returns:
      critical pressure ratio r_c

    • estimateVapourPressureBara

      private double estimateVapourPressureBara(SystemInterface sys)
      Estimate vapour pressure of the liquid at inlet temperature using a bubble-point flash on a clone of the system. Returns NaN on failure.
      Parameters:
      sys - inlet system
      Returns:
      vapour pressure in bara, or NaN if it could not be computed
